frontmatter.description: A container exited with reason OOMKilled — memory limit exceeded

signature.embedding_seed: pod oom killed memory limit exceeded container

body:
## When this fires
The Linux OOM killer terminated the container because RSS exceeded the configured memory limit. Common causes: a leak, a sudden batch with a larger working set, or a too-tight limit for the workload.

## Playbook
1. Run pod-describe to confirm OOMKilled and capture the limits.
2. Plot memory-trend across recent restarts to see whether it is a leak (monotonic) or a spike (saw-tooth).
3. Read recent-deploys-svc for changes that could have shifted the working set.
